displayTitle: Made, Not Mined: Birth of a Gamma Source

beats.narration: Nobody mines iridium one ninety two. Every gamma source begins as stable metal — radioactivity we manufacture on purpose.

payload.title: MADE, NOT MINED
payload.sub: How radiographic gamma sources are manufactured.

beats.narration: Neutron activation: in the reactor, a stable nucleus captures one neutron — same element, one mass number heavier, pushed off the stable band.

payload.heading: Neutron activation: one neutron in
payload.note: MASS +1 · SAME ELEMENT · OFF THE STABLE BAND

beats.narration: Radiography's workhorses are activation products: cobalt sixty, iridium one ninety two, selenium seventy five — each one neutron heavier than its stable target.
Route two is fission: uranium two thirty five splits in two — neutron-rich fragments, born radioactive. Cesium one thirty seven is the classic.
Once it leaves the reactor, every source is decaying toward stability — always on. There's no off switch, only shielding and time.
